message.content:
TM Flow Script (Techman Robot) 공식 문법 문서를 찾아줘. 특히:
1. Vision 함수 호출 문법
2. Move 함수 문법 (MoveL, MoveJ 등)
3. I/O 제어 문법
4. 변수 선언/사용법
5. 루프/조건문 문법

공식 매뉴얼 PDF가 있으면 URL 찾아주고, 가능하면 C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\ 폴더에 다운로드해줘.

content.text: Techman Robot TM Flow Script 공식 문서를 검색하겠습니다.

input.query: Techman Robot TM Flow Script programming manual PDF official
테크맨 로봇 TM Flow Script 문법 매뉴얼

(experimental) (not support the new TM AI Cobot S Series)","url":"https://github.com/TechmanRobotInc/tmr_ros1"},{"title":"TMflow | Techman Robot","url":"https://www.tm-robot.com/en/tmflow/"},{"title":"5. ROS1 Demonstration — TM ROS v1 documentation","url":"https://techman-robot-ros.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ROS1Demonstration.html"},{"title":"Free AI Translator - Best Translation from 22 Models","url":"https://www.machinetranslation.com/"}]

검색 결과를 찾았습니다. TMscript는 TM AI 협동로봇을 위해 설계된 프로그래밍 언어로, TMflow의 Flow 프로젝트와 Script 프로젝트 모두에 적용되며 사용자 친화성과 고급 기능 사이의 균형을 맞추고 있습니다.

공식 Techman Robot 웹사이트의 TMscript 페이지(https://www.tm-robot.com/en/tmscript/)에서 TM Flow Script 문법에 대한 더 상세한 정보와 매뉴얼을 찾을 수 있을 것으로 보입니다. 또한 TMflow는 직관적인 소프트웨어로 일련의 함수 노드를 통해 로봇 작업 생성의 복잡성을 단순한 경험으로 변환합니다.

더 자세한 문법 매뉴얼은 공식 Techman Robot 문서 페이지에서 확인하시는 것을 권장합니다.

Based on the search results, I found several relevant resources for Techman Robot TM Flow Script programming:

## Key Findings:

1. **Official Resources**: Techman Robot's documentation includes support for Lua Script Programming, and there are documentation archives available at the official Techman Robot website.

2. **Programming Language**: TMscript is a programming language designed for TM AI Cobots, applicable to both Flow projects and Script projects in TMflow.

3. **Documentation Available**: Users can download the Expression Editor Manual from TM Download Center or contact Techman Robot directly for the most current documentation.

4. **PDF Manual Link**: A TMflow Software Manual PDF was found at: `https://accu-techusa.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Software-Manual-TMflow_SW1.76_Rev1.00_EN.pdf` (though this appears to be an older version from 2021).

5. **Official Site**: TMflow is designed with user-friendly design, making it easy to master flow-based programming without any robotic experience.

For the most current and official TM Flow Script programming manual PDF, I recommend visiting the **official Techman Robot website** at `www.tm-robot.com/en/tmflow/` or their documentation download center, as the manuals are regularly updated and the versions available online may vary.

content.content:
# TMscript | Techman Robot

Based on the webpage content provided, here's what I can tell you about TMscript:

## Overview
TMscript is a programming language specifically designed for TM AI Cobots. According to the site, it's "applicable to both Flow projects and Script projects in TMflow, striking a remarkable balance between user-friendliness and advanced functionality."

## Key Information
The page indicates this is documentation for Techman Robot's scripting solution, with the URL pointing to their TMscript resource page (tm-robot.com/en/tmscript/).

## What's Available
The page structure suggests access to:
- Programming language documentation
- Integration with TMflow projects
- Resources for both beginners and advanced users

## Note
The webpage content provided is primarily composed of technical markup, styling code, and configuration data rather than detailed TMscript documentation. For comprehensive information about TMscript syntax, functions, and usage examples, you would need to visit the actual Techman Robot website or access their downloadable manuals through their documentation portal.

If you're looking for specific TMscript syntax or function details, visiting the main Techman Robot website directly would provide more detailed technical resources.

# TMflow Archives Page Analysis

Based on the page content, this appears to be a documentation archive page for TMflow on the Techman Robot website. The page is built using Elementor and includes various documentation management features.

## Key Technical Elements

The page implements several modern web technologies:

- **Performance optimization**: RocketLazyLoadScripts class handles lazy loading of scripts based on user interaction
- **Analytics**: Google Analytics (GA-VBC6RLLV54) and Google Tag Manager (GTM-TSHD7T3) integration
- **Search functionality**: BetterDocs plugin provides "live search" capabilities for documentation
- **Schema markup**: Organization and WebSite schema data for SEO

## Documentation Features

The page appears to support:
- Categorized documentation browsing
- Search filtering for TMflow-related content
- Multiple layout options for content display
- Responsive design across device sizes

## Content Organization

The styling and structure suggest a professional documentation hub with categories, tags, and hierarchical navigation systems for accessing TMflow-related resources and guides.

**Note**: The actual TMflow manual PDF download links themselves are not visible in the provided HTML/CSS content snippet.

(experimental) (support the new TM AI Cobot S Series)","url":"https://github.com/TechmanRobotInc/tm2_ros1"},{"title":"GitHub - TechmanRobotInc/tm2_ros2: TM Robots supporting ROS2 drivers and some extended external applications. (experimental) (support the new TM AI Cobot S Series)","url":"https://github.com/TechmanRobotInc/tm2_ros2"},{"title":"Expression Editor Manual_SW1.88 Archives | Techman Robot","url":"https://www.tm-robot.com/en/wp-file-download/expression-editor-manual_sw1-88/"},{"title":"TMscript Archives | Techman Robot","url":"https://www.tm-robot.com/en/wp-file-download/tmscript/"},{"title":"Library component - RoboDK","url":"https://robodk.com/post/Techman-TMscript"},{"title":"Expression Editor Archives | Techman Robot","url":"https://www.tm-robot.com/en/wp-file-download/expression-editor/"},{"title":"TMflow | Techman Robot","url":"https://www.tm-robot.com/en/tmflow/"},{"title":"Software Manual TMflow - GitHub","url":"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wiki/jvdtoorn/techmanpy/resources/tmflow-manual.pdf"}]

Based on the search results, I found several relevant resources for Techman Robot TMscript documentation:

## Key Resources Found

1. **TMscript Language Manual PDF**: The Omron TM Collaborative Robot TMscript Language Manual is available as a PDF, which can be accessed at: https://files.omron.eu/downloads/latest/manual/en/i664_tm_collaborative_robot_tmscript_language_manual_users_manual_en.pdf

2. **Official TMscript Documentation**: TMscript is a programming language designed for TM AI Cobots, applicable to both Flow projects and Script projects in TMflow. The official page is available at https://www.tm-robot.com/en/tmscript/

3. **Expression Editor Manual**: Users can download the "Expression Editor Manual" from the TM Download Center, which covers the expression editor functionality including commands like Move, MoveL, and MoveJ.

4. **GitHub Documentation**: For using TMscript expressions and Listen Node commands, you can refer to the Manual: Programming Language TMscript, which is referenced in the official Techman Robot GitHub repositories.

## What These Resources Cover

The TMscript Language Manual includes documentation on:
- Programming functions for robot control (including movement commands)
- Variable declarations and data types
- Expressions and function syntax
- Vision-related operations

For direct downloads and the latest versions of these manuals, you may want to visit the TM Download Center or contact Techman Robot directly.

Based on the search results, I found information about TMscript from Techman Robot. Here's what I discovered:

## TMscript Language Overview

TMscript is a programming language designed for TM AI Cobots. TMscript is the programming language of Techman Robot applicable to Flow projects and Script projects.

## Variable Declaration and Data Types

Different data types of variables can be declared in Variable Manager. Regarding specific data types mentioned in your query:

- **Integer Types**: In function terms, the integer type further goes by int16 and int32, with int32 as the default.
- **String Handling**: Without double quotation marks, strings will be taken as variables. Additionally, string constants need to be enclosed in double quotes.

The search results indicate that the official TMscript Language Manual is available from Omron/Techman Robot, but the full detailed syntax for MoveL and MoveJ functions with specific float and string variable examples wasn't fully extracted from these results. For comprehensive details on MoveL/MoveJ syntax with concrete examples, I recommend accessing the official TMscript Language Manual directly from the Omron files link found in the search results.

I found several relevant GitHub repositories and documentation related to Techman Robot. The search results point to multiple resources about TMflow, TMscript, and Vision nodes. Here's what I found:

## Key Resources on GitHub

The TechmanRobotInc GitHub repositories support External TM ROS Driver packages, and include documentation for TMflow, Vision Nodes, and the Programming Language TMscript.

## TMscript Language

TMscript is a programming language designed for TM AI Cobots, applicable to both Flow projects and Script projects in TMflow. The Script Node and Script Project features allow for more flexible and detailed programming for complex application needs.

## Vision Functionality

The Vision node provides the creation of a plane with fixed-point type, servo type, and object type as well as a variety of AOI identification functions.

## Relevant GitHub Repositories

The main GitHub organizations include:
- **TechmanRobotInc/tm2_ros2** and **TechmanRobotInc/tm2_ros1** - For ROS2 and ROS1 drivers (TM 2.14+)
- **TechmanRobotInc/tmr_ros2** and **TechmanRobotInc/tmr_ros1** - For earlier ROS implementations

For specific syntax examples and detailed programming documentation, you should refer to the Manual: Programming Language TMscript for more details.

# RoboDK Library Component Overview

Based on the webpage content provided, this appears to be a RoboDK library documentation page focused on **TMscript syntax examples** including movement commands, vision systems, input/output control, variables, loops, and conditional statements.

## Key Information

**What RoboDK Offers:**
RoboDK describes itself as "a complete software solution for robot simulation and offline programming of robot arms." The platform supports various manufacturing applications including welding, machining, polishing, and palletizing.

**Available Resources:**
The site provides access to comprehensive documentation, API references, and a search tool. Users can explore practical applications through examples and benefit from community forums and blog content.

**Support & Documentation:**
The organization offers technical support at "info@robodk.com" and "+1-855-692-7772," alongside extensive online resources including quick help guides, tips and tricks sections, and an academy for learning.

The page emphasizes RoboDK's compatibility with multiple robot types and CAD/CAM software integration for flexible offline programming workflows.

I found search results related to TMflow and Techman Robot on GitHub. The results show information about TM ROS drivers and TMflow projects, but they don't contain specific syntax examples for the Script node commands you're looking for (MoveL, MoveJ, SetDO, Vision_Run).

The search results indicate that:

- The TM ROS driver sends the robot script (TM Robot Expression) to control robot locomotion and IO
- TMscript is the programming language of Techman Robot applicable to Flow projects and Script projects
- Users can establish a socket TCP listener in the listen node to communicate based on packet format, and all features available in TM ROBOT Function can be operated in the listen node

However, the search results reference documentation like "Expression Editor and Listen Node.pdf" that would contain detailed syntax examples, but those specific syntax examples are not included in the GitHub search results returned. You may need to consult the official Techman Robot documentation or look for repositories that contain example scripts or documentation PDFs with the specific syntax for MoveL, MoveJ, SetDO, and Vision_Run commands.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

(experimental) (not support the new TM AI Cobot S Series)","url":"https://github.com/TechmanRobotInc/tmr_ros1"}]

Based on the search results, I found some relevant information about Techman TMflow Listen Node scripting. Here's what I found:

Examples of QueueTag syntax in TM listen node scripts show commands like "QueueTag(1)\r" or "QueueTag(2)\n", which demonstrates how the QueueTag function is used in script commands.

The TMSCT command format includes syntax like `TMSCT << ID{"1"} << QueueTag(1, 1) << End() << QueueTag(1, 1)`, showing how QueueTag can be used with the TMSCT command structure.

TMSTA command syntax includes examples like `TMSTA << QueueTag(1, 1) << End()` and `TMSTA << QueueTagDone(1) << ScriptExit()`, demonstrating the format for status response commands.

All functions available in Expression Editor can be executed in Listen Node, though the search results don't provide specific details about MoveL and MoveJ syntax.

To get more detailed information about the complete TMSCT and TMSTA format specifications, MoveL/MoveJ syntax, and other advanced scripting features, you may want to consult the official Techman Robot documentation or Expression Editor Manual directly, as the search results reference these documents but don't provide the full syntax details.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "TMflow expression editor manual "var" OR "int" OR "float" OR "string" variable declaration syntax example"

I found documentation from the TMflow Expression Editor manual. Based on the search results, here's the relevant information about variable declaration syntax:

Variable types supported include byte (8-bit unsigned), int (32-bit signed), float (32-bit floating-point), double (64-bit floating-point), and bool (boolean). For int type variables, both int16 and int32 are supported, with int32 being the default type.

The manuals indicate examples of variable usage with type declarations. For example, float var_f = (float) var_dvalue shows float type declaration with type casting. For byte variables, the syntax example shows: byte var_b = -100 // var_b = 156.

In the naming rule of variables, only numbers, underscores, and upper and lower case English characters are supported.

The search results come from the official Omron/Techman Robot TMflow Expression Editor and Listen Node Reference Manual (Version 1.84).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

   198→Screwdriver Position to Execute Commands
   199→To successfully execute the Screwdriver commands, it is fundamental to position the
   200→Screwdriver correctly. This is achieved if the following two conditions are met:
   201→1. The Screw-bit System must be perfectly aligned to
   202→the screw or thread.
   203→데이터 시트
   204→28
   205→
   206→=== 페이지 29 ===
   207→2. The distance between the Screwdriver's bottom part
   208→and the surface where the action takes place must
   209→be within the range of 0-8 mm [0-0.31 inches].
   210→To successfully execute the Screwdriver commands, it is fundamental to operate the
   211→Screwdriver downwards or maximum sidewards. The Screwdriver should not be operated
   212→upwards or with an angle higher than 90° orientate with respect to the ground, since this will
   213→trigger the protective feature.
   214→LED - Device Status
   215→The screwdriver has a LED that shows the device status.
   216→데이터 시트
   217→29
   218→
   219→=== 페이지 30 ===
   220→Color
   221→Device Status
   222→ No light
   223→Power missing
   224→ Steady green
   225→Ready to work - Idle - Static
   226→ Blinking green
   227→Initializing
   228→ Steady orange
   229→Busy – Moving/rotating shank
   230→ Blinking orange
   231→Operational malfunction
   232→ Steady red
   233→Not working – Hardware problem
   234→ Blinking red
   235→Safety – Emergency stop
   236→Torque Angle Curve and Torque Gradient
   237→The torque gradient shows how the torque is applied in the last phase of the Tightening
   238→screw command. This could be used as an indicator to detect if a Tightening command is
   239→performed correctly.
   240→참고
   241→When using self-tapping screws, if the tapping torque is very close to the
   242→target torque, the torque gradient might provide an invalid value.
   243→For instance, the torque gradient could be different if:
   244→•
   245→The hole thread is not long enough
   246→•
   247→The hole thread is different from the screw thread
   248→•
   249→The hole thread is not clean (for instance by deburrs from CNC machining)
   250→•
   251→The friction between the screw thread and the hole thread is too low or too high
   252→•
   253→The friction between the screw head and the tighten part is too low or too high
   254→A torque gradient variable is made available to be checked in the robot program.
   255→The graph below shows a normal Torque/Angle curve. In this case has been made with an
   256→M4 screw and 2.4 Nm as target torque.
   257→데이터 시트
   258→30
   259→
   260→=== 페이지 31 ===
   261→Torque angle curve Metric
   262→Torque angle curve US Standard
   263→Adjusting the Bellow back in Place
   264→참고
   265→Initially, the bellow should not come out of place, but if it does, follow the
   266→instructions below to adjust it back in place.
   267→데이터 시트
   268→31
   269→
   270→=== 페이지 32 ===
   271→1. Remove the lid.
   272→2. Move the shank to the highest possible
   273→value by using the user interface in the
   274→robot or in the Web Client.
   275→3. Adjust the bellow back in place.
   276→4. Place the lid back on.
   277→데이터 시트

cd "C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/DAEHYUN_PRIVATE/TM Robot" && curl -L -o "TMscript_Language_Manual_Omron_EN.pdf" "https://files.omron.eu/downloads/latest/manual/en/i664_tm_collaborative_robot_tmscript_language_manual_users_manual_en.pdf?v=4" --max-time 120 -w "
HTTP Status: %{http_code}
Size: %{size_download} bytes
" 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Omron TMscript Language Manual PDF 다운로드

content.text: 4개 PDF 모두 다운로드 완료. 이제 TMscript 매뉴얼에서 핵심 문법을 추출하겠습니다.

input.command:
cd "C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/DAEHYUN_PRIVATE/TM Robot" && python -c "
import subprocess, sys
# pdftotext로 TMscript 매뉴얼 텍스트 추출 시도
result = subprocess.run(['python', '-c', 'import pdfminer; print(\"ok\")'], capture_output=True, text=True)
print('pdfminer:', result.stdout.strip(), result.stderr.strip()[:50])
" 2>&1

python -c "
from pdfminer.high_level import extract_text
text = extract_text('C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/DAEHYUN_PRIVATE/TM Robot/TMscript_Language_Manual_Omron_EN.pdf')
print(repr(text[:500]))
print('---')
print('Total length:', len(text))
" 2>&1
